request.system: You are a regulatory analyst. Write a 2-3 sentence storyline summary (≤500 chars total) of the regulatory pressure history for this prediction-market contract. For 'retrospective' kind, write past tense and reference the actual resolution. For 'active' kind, write present tense and note what's still pending. Return JSON {text: ...}.
request.user:
Contract: Will US tariffs on Chinese goods exceed 60% average effective rate in 2026?
Kind: active
Timeline (5 events):
- 2026-05-22 [background] FIL-24-2026 - 22.05.2026 - Notice of Proposed Rulemaking to Establish Bank Secrecy Act and Sanctions Compliance Standards for FDIC-Supervised Permitted Payment Stablecoin Issuers — FDIC proposed rule on stablecoin BSA/sanctions compliance does not affect USTR or Commerce tariff determinations on China.
- 2026-05-21 [background] OCC Announces Enforcement Actions for May 2026 — OCC enforcement actions concern bank supervision and do not bear on USTR or Commerce tariff determinations for China.
- 2026-04-27 [background] READOUT: Secretary of the Treasury Scott Bessent’s Meeting with European Commissioner for Trade and Economic Security Maroš Šefčovič — Treasury–EU trade readout contains no indication USTR/Commerce will set US–China average tariff >60%; negligible effect on resolution odds.
- 2026-04-24 [background] Economic Fury Targets Global Network Fueling Iran’s Oil Trade and Shadow Fleet — OFAC sanctions target Iran-related shipping/oil firms and do not affect USTR/Commerce tariff determinations, so they have negligible effect on a >60% China tariff outcome.
- 2026-01-29 [background] Treasury Releases Report on Macroeconomic and Foreign Exchange Policies of Major Trading Partners of the United States — Treasury macro/FX report does not announce USTR or Commerce tariff findings; at best a background policy signal with negligible effect on a >60% tariff outcome.

response.text: Recent actions (FDIC stablecoin compliance NPRM, OCC enforcement, Treasury–EU trade readout, Iran-related OFAC sanctions, and Treasury FX report) are peripheral to tariff-setting and offer no signal that average tariffs on China will exceed 60%. There is no direct USTR or Commerce move toward such levels; key 2026 tariff determinations remain pending.
